concerns.text: Tasks T017a, T017b, and T017d implement a 'DriftModel' and generate 'trajectories' (sequences of steps). The Spec (FR-001) and Plan (Phase 1.1) explicitly require generating '10,000 synthetic attention matrices' (static). The tasks do not cover the Spec's requirement for static matrices; instead, they implement a scope-creep methodology (trajectories) not authorized by the Spec. There is no task covering the generation of the 10,000 static matrices required by FR-001.

Tasks T022 and T010 implement an MLP using 4 moments (mean, variance, skewness, kurtosis). Spec FR-002 explicitly restricts inputs to 'the first two moments (mean, variance)'. The Plan (Phase 2.1) also explicitly excludes higher-order moments. There is no task covering the implementation of the MLP with the Spec-compliant 2-moment input.

Task T017d requires verifying a row count of '10,000 trajectories'. Spec FR-001 requires generating '10,000 synthetic attention matrices'. If the task generates trajectories (sequences), the count metric does not map to the Spec's requirement of 10,000 individual matrices. No task exists to verify the generation of 10,000 static matrices.

Task T041 is marked 'OUT OF SCOPE' and describes a placeholder for 'Real-world data extraction'. While the description correctly notes it is excluded, the task remains in the active task list with a checkbox. This is a stale/placeholder task that should be removed to prevent confusion, as it references a requirement (FR-010/SC-007) that does not exist in the current Spec.
